channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: FaZe Suffer Another Painful Exit at ESL Pro League Season 23
item.content:encoded:
FaZe Clan have suffered another exit as their ESL Pro League Season 23 run was ended by Astralis in a close 2-1 defeat which saw their dramatic Map 3 comeback fall painfully short.

FaZe were defeated 7-13, 13-3, 16-14 on Dust 2, Ancient, and Inferno in the 1-2 bracket to exit Pro League in 12th-14th in Stage 2, the second online stage of the event.

The exit is another in a long string of sorry results for one of Counter-Strike’s most celebrated and decorated teams, a streak only interrupted by a miraculous Grand Final run at the StarLadder Budapest 2025 Major.

FaZe adjustments not enough to stop rot

FaZe’s run at Pro League was ended by Astralis following losses to G2 and paiN, with FaZe’s only win coming against Monte, who had advanced from Stage 1 of the competition.

The loss will be a bitter pill to swallow, as Astralis made two changes coming into the year, adding Love ‘phzy’ Smidebrant and Gytis ‘ryu’ Glušauskas, switching comms to English. FaZe, by contrast, made zero changes in the off-season, but is seemingly still struggling to find its map pool, positions, and balance in the roster.

The run continued a series of disappointing results since veteran FaZe rifler Håvard ‘rain’ Nygaard left the organisation in October last year.

FaZe tournament results since rain’s departure:

  • CS Asia Championships 2025: 7th-8th
  • IEM Chengdu 2025: 13th-16th
  • StarLadder Budapest Major 2025: 2nd
  • BLAST Bounty 2026 Season 1: 17th-32nd
  • IEM Kraków 2026: 9th-12th
  • PGL Cluj-Napoca 2026: 12th-14th
  • ESL Pro League Season 23 Stage 2: 12th-14th

While many of the above events saw FaZe stubbornly plough on despite poor events, Pro League saw FaZe make several adjustments in an attempt to change their fate.

FaZe have now moved to Dust 2 as their ‘home’ map, picking it in all series in Pro League besides the Monte match, where Monte picked the map first. Dust 2 gave FaZe wins against paiN, Monte, and Astralis, though it also saw them lose 13-1 in their opening Pro League showing against G2.

Additionally, FaZe are now targeting Overpass, a map they notably struggled on in Budapest despite the strong result there. FaZe defeated Monte on the map 13-11, in a series which saw them pick the map.

The win followed an adjustment at Cluj-Napoca, which saw Russel ‘Twistzz’ Van Dulken and David ‘frozen’ Čerňanský swap positions, with Twistzz taking the more proactive A Bathrooms role and frozen moving to B Rotate.

Whether Overpass is ultimately a strong map for FaZe is still unclear, but the swap certainly improved their CT-side balance, a vital aspect of the largely defence-favoured map.

It was the rest of the map pool that let FaZe down in the end, losing Ancient twice, Inferno once, and suffering defeats on Nuke and Mirage to paiN.

After almost 200 days together as this five-man roster, it seems that FaZe can still not find the right balance in their roster across the six maps they need to play to compete. Taking energy to focus on Overpass and Dust 2 may have led them to neglect issues elsewhere.

Indeed, focus on preparation was cited by Twistzz as a reason for his individual form looking relatively weak at Pro League, posting, “Went in the right direction with the prep I do, but lost some of myself individually.”

The Canadian averaged only a 0.93 HLTV rating at the event, but in truth, Twistzz, alongside frozen, are the least of FaZe’s problems. The question looms large, ‘Can this roster work?’

You can discuss that question and more on Insider Gaming’s Discord Server.

For more Counter-Strike esports news, have you seen that jL is standing in for Liquid at Roman Imperium Cup VI?

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fallout 3 Remastered Rumours Resurface With New Toy Listing
item.content:encoded:
A new toy listing features an item from the rumoured Fallout 3 Remastered title.

Rumours about a remastered version of Fallout 3 have been circulating for quite some time now. Some insiders have claimed that it is indeed in the works, but won’t be released anytime soon. Moreover, the suspected studio has refuted working on the rumoured remasters. However, new evidence has now popped up.

Fallout 3 Remastered ‘Nuka Cola’ Mentioned in New Toy Listing

Spotted on r/Fallout, a new toy figure listing has surfaced online for a range of new McFarlane Toys. McFarlane Toys is an American company known for creating action figures based on movies, comics, and games. The listing features a range of new DC toys, Transformers, Marvel, and more. Hidden within the list are the Elite Edition toys, which feature the highly anticipated Fallout 3 Remastered.

There are 3 Elite Edition toys that appeared in the listing, and the one that will catch everybody’s eye is ‘Fallout 3 Remastered – #13 T-45B Nuka Cola’. The listing is yet to be officially confirmed by McFarlane Toys; however, we did see the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake surface as a figurine. Additionally, it is worth noting that Fallout 3 didn’t have any Nuka-Cola-themed power armor sets, and the T-45b was in Fallout 4, not Fallout 3. Regardless, players should take this information with a grain of salt.

Other than Fallout 3, there are several other video-game-themed action figurines mentioned in the listing. First up is DC Multiverse’s Batman from Arkham Knight. Then we have an Elite Edition Hellidvers 2 Combat Technician. Lastly, towards the end, the list also mentions “Marvel Games Collection 1:10th – Wolverine (Wolverine Game Ver.) (Gold Label) and players are speculating whether this figurine is from Insominac’s Marvel’s Wolverine.

In other news, Fallout: The New West Preview release trailer has surfaced online. Additionally, Fallout 4 re-entered the best-seller charts last month, ten years after launch. What are your thoughts on Fallout 3 Remastered being mentioned in a toy listing? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Resident Evil Requiem director confirming a new story expansion. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Story Expansion Confirmed by Director
item.content:encoded:
Resident Evil Requiem’s director has confirmed that a bunch of new content is planned for the game, along with a story expansion.

Capcom’s latest instalment in the iconic survival horror franchise has been faring quite well since launch, becoming the franchise’s fastest-selling game ever and one of the greatest games of all time, according to Metacritic user scores. To celebrate the game’s success, the developers have made a few announcements for players.

Resident Evil Requiem Sells Over 5 Million Copies in 2 Weeks; New Content and Expansion Announced

A new post on the official RE_Games account on X (formerly Twitter) confirms the new story expansion for Resident Evil Requiem. The game’s director, Koshi Nakanishi, revealed that they have already sold more than 5 million copies since launch on February 27, 2026. The director revealed that the development team will continue to address all bugs and performance issues the game faces, and also confirmed new content.

Nakanishi revealed that Resident Evil Requiem will receive a photo mode soon, the developers have a surprise planned for May 2026, and they also have a new mini-game on the way. The director then confirmed that extra story content is now in development for the title, and the expansion will “delve deeper into the world of Requiem”. No release date was revealed, but the developers have stated that “it will take some time”.

In other news, Grace Ashcroft star has praised the voice actor behind Leon Kennedy’s impressive performances. Additionally, here are the 14 best mods that you need to download for the game. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil Requiem’s story expansion being confirmed? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Donald Glover voicing Yoshi in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Tuesday, March 10
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Tuesday, March 10, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 10)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 10, 2026.

Across

1 – Writing that lacks substance

6 – Pencil in a cosmetics bag

7 – ___ acid (building block of proteins)

8 – Partner or services, in economics

9 – Small criticism

Down

1 – Warning sign in a relationship, metaphorically

2 – Fancy prom ride

3 – SAG-AFTRA, for one

4 – Luxury fashion house headquartered in Rome

5 – Ground coating on a cold morning

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 10)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 10, 2026.

Across

1 – Small bits of cotton

6 – Like a cruise ship

7 – NH2

8 – Shop owner’s stock

9 – Slang for head lice

Down

1 – Red colour object waved to signal a stoppage in motor racing

2 – Stretched vehicle

3 – Flag of Great Britain, ___ Jack

4 – Major Prada rival

5 – Nick ___, regular co-star to Simon Pegg

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 10)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 10, 2026.

Across

1 – FLUFF

6 – LINER

7 – AMINO

8 – GOODS

9 – NIT

Down

1 – FLAG

2 – LIMO

3 – UNION

4 – FENDI

5 – FROST

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more word game hints, check out today’s Connections, and keep an eye for more guides published on Insider Gaming!

MOBILE APP
